data.title: Dynasty Fine Wines Flags Sharp 2025 Profit Drop on Weak China Wine Demand
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Dynasty Fine Wines Group Limited ( (HK:0828) ) has provided an announcement.

Dynasty Fine Wines Group Limited has warned that profit attributable to shareholders for 2025 is expected to fall sharply to between HK$11.7 million and HK$15.0 million, down about 55% to 65% from HK$33.4 million a year earlier. The decline is driven mainly by a 35% to 38% drop in revenue to about HK$168 million to HK$175 million, reflecting weaker wine demand in China, particularly for medium- to high-end products, and higher loss allowances for trade receivables.

The company said the impact was partially offset by a net gain from a compensatory surrender booked during the year and stressed that the figures are based on unaudited management accounts, pending final audit adjustments. In response to challenging macroeconomic conditions and soft consumption, Dynasty is adjusting its strategy by reinforcing its core markets with premium offerings, tapping into low-alcohol and youth-oriented segments, expanding online sales channels, and tightening cost controls to mitigate operational pressures.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0828) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.26 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Dynasty Fine Wines Group Limited stock, see the HK:0828 Stock Forecast page.

More about Dynasty Fine Wines Group Limited

Dynasty Fine Wines Group Limited is a Hong Kong-listed wine producer operating in the PRC, focusing on the manufacture and sale of medium- to high-end wines. The Group targets the Chinese wine consumption market and leverages its established brands while expanding online channels and developing products aimed at low-alcohol and younger consumer segments.

Average Trading Volume: 256,171

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$333.8M

Learn more about 0828 stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: FWD Group Sets Formal Mandate for Nomination and Corporate Governance Committee
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

FWD Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:1828) ) has provided an update.

FWD Group Holdings Limited has formalised the terms of reference for its Nomination and Corporate Governance Committee, a board-level body created in February 2022 to oversee nomination practices and corporate governance across the group. The committee is mandated to review board structure and composition, recommend director appointments and committee memberships, assess independence of non-executive directors, oversee board evaluations, and manage succession planning for the chairman, Group CEO, senior management, and key control function holders.

The committee is also tasked with overseeing the company’s ESG and sustainability strategy, policies, and performance, as well as broader corporate governance policies and practices. It is empowered to obtain information from directors and executives, access independent professional advice at the company’s expense, and engage search firms and external experts, underscoring FWD’s effort to institutionalise robust governance and ESG oversight that should enhance transparency and stakeholder confidence as the group grows.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1828) stock is a Buy with a HK$50.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on FWD Group Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:1828 Stock Forecast page.

More about FWD Group Holdings Limited

FWD Group Holdings Limited is an insurance group headquartered in Hong Kong, operating through a corporate structure that includes a board of directors and executive committee members. The company focuses on governance frameworks, regulatory compliance under Hong Kong’s Insurance Ordinance, and adherence to Hong Kong Stock Exchange listing rules, reflecting a strong emphasis on oversight and risk control across its subsidiaries.

Average Trading Volume: 667,103

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

data.title: Standard Chartered advances buy-back with US$191m deployed and share count cut
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Standard Chartered ( (GB:STAN) ) has provided an announcement.

Standard Chartered has continued executing its share buy-back programme, repurchasing 900,000 ordinary shares on 12 March 2026 through J.P. Morgan Securities across the London Stock Exchange and CBOE venues at a volume-weighted average price of about 1,609 GB pence. The bank has now deployed more than US$191 million on the buy-back, and plans to cancel the repurchased shares, reducing the share count to 2.24 billion and marginally increasing earnings per share and voting power for remaining shareholders.

The latest tranche of buy-backs underscores management’s ongoing capital-return strategy and signals confidence in the group’s balance sheet and future earnings capacity. By shrinking its equity base while operating within pre-set regulatory parameters, Standard Chartered is aligning with peers in using excess capital to enhance shareholder value, which may support the stock’s valuation in a competitive global banking sector.

More about Standard Chartered

Standard Chartered PLC is a London-headquartered international banking group incorporated in England and Wales, listed in Hong Kong and other major markets. The bank provides a broad range of financial services, with a strong presence in Asia, Africa and the Middle East, and operates through retail, commercial and institutional banking franchises focused on cross-border and emerging markets.

data.title: 160 Health Controlling Shareholders Extend Lock-Up on 30.59% Stake
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

160 Health International Ltd. ( (HK:2656) ) has issued an update.

160 Health International Limited has announced that its controlling shareholders, including chairman and CEO Luo Ningzheng and related entities, have voluntarily extended their lock-up commitment on 102,912,905 shares, or about 30.59% of the company’s issued share capital. The extended lock-up, now running through 16 September 2026, means these shareholders will not dispose of any of their relevant shares during this period, aligning their restriction terms with those applicable to other locked-up shareholders and signaling a stronger commitment to the company’s stability and investor confidence.

The move effectively lengthens the period during which the controlling shareholders are barred from selling or encumbering their holdings, beyond the original first six‑month post‑listing lock‑up set out under Hong Kong listing rules. By voluntarily tightening their own liquidity, the controlling shareholders are likely aiming to support the company’s share price performance, reduce overhang risk in the market, and reinforce their long-term alignment with minority investors amid the early phase of the company’s life as a listed entity.

More about 160 Health International Ltd.

160 Health International Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 2656. The company operates in the healthcare sector, with controlling shareholders holding a significant minority stake that positions them as key long-term stakeholders in its capital structure and governance.

data.title: Shanghai Industrial Urban Development Buys RMB2.7 Billion Mixed-Use Site in Shanghai
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Shanghai Industrial Urban Development Group Ltd ( (HK:0563) ) has provided an announcement.

Shanghai Industrial Urban Development Group has acquired land use rights for a mixed-use site in Xuhui District, Shanghai, for approximately RMB2.675 billion through a government public auction. The parcel, near existing and planned metro lines between the city’s Middle and Outer Ring roads, covers 27,570.79 square meters and allows a gross floor area of about 55,141.58 square meters, implying an average land cost of RMB48,511 per square meter.

The site is designated for 75% residential and 25% commercial development, with the retail portion aimed at serving local community needs and enhancing the value of the residential units. The company expects both the sale of apartments and long-term operation of commercial facilities to provide stable cash flow, reinforcing its strategic land reserves in Shanghai and supporting its ongoing sustainable growth in the ordinary course of business.

Ever Harvest Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:1549) ) has provided an announcement.

Ever Harvest Group Holdings has issued a positive profit alert, indicating it expects to swing to a profit attributable to equity holders of about HK$7.7 million for the year ended 31 December 2025, compared with a loss of approximately HK$9.3 million a year earlier. The improvement underscores a recovery in its operations, supported by higher shipment volumes, increased government grants and the removal of prior-year tax penalties, which together mark a notable turnaround in the company’s earnings trajectory.

The board cited a roughly 12% rise in revenue, or about HK$47 million, driven mainly by increased shipment volume, as a key driver of the expected results, alongside supportive government funding and cleaner tax charges. While the figures are based on unaudited management accounts and may change when the final 2025 annual results are published in late March 2026, the alert signals a strengthening financial position that could reshape investor expectations and the company’s standing in its market.

Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing ( (HK:0388) ) has shared an announcement.

Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing Limited (HKEX), the operator of Hong Kong’s stock and derivatives markets, has scheduled a Board meeting for 29 April 2026 to review and approve the release of its unaudited consolidated results for the quarter ended 31 March 2026. The planned results announcement will give investors and other stakeholders an early view of HKEX’s financial and operational performance for the start of 2026, potentially shaping market expectations about trading activity and earnings momentum at the exchange.

The company also confirmed that its Board is currently composed of 12 independent non-executive directors and one executive director, who also serves as chief executive, underscoring a governance structure with a strong independent majority. This board composition, together with the regular disclosure of quarterly performance, is likely to be scrutinised by market participants assessing HKEX’s corporate governance standards and strategic direction in a period of evolving market conditions.

More about Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing

Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing Limited (HKEX) operates as the primary securities and derivatives exchange operator in Hong Kong, running the stock and futures markets and related clearing and settlement infrastructure. The group focuses on providing listing, trading, and post-trade services for equities and other financial instruments, serving as a key platform for capital raising and investment in the Hong Kong and regional markets.
